sentence: The patient, Theresa, is a 30-year-old female who presents with a complaint of having had headaches for about a month, which are described as throbbing and occur on the top of her head.
She reports that the headaches are accompanied by dizziness and blurry vision.
She also mentions that her blood pressure has been running high.
She has been taking chlorthalidone for hypertension but has not been taking it as prescribed due to stomachache.
She admits to having a history of diabetes and is currently taking metformin, but has not been taking it as directed.
She consumes a diet high in sodium, often eating at fast food restaurants like McDonald's.
She reports that her blood sugar has been running high, with a reading of 170 this morning, and attributes this to consuming Easter candy over the weekend.
The patient has a history of hypertension and diabetes.
The patient reports experiencing dizziness and blurry vision with her headaches.
I prescribed chlorthalidone 50mg daily for hypertension.
She is also taking metformin, but has not been taking it as directed.
Extremities: 1+ pitting edema on the legs bilaterally
Assessment: The patient has uncontrolled hypertension and diabetes, with a blood sugar reading of 170 mg/dL.
Problem list: Hypertension active; diabetes active.
I want to continue her current medication regimen, including chlorthalidone and metformin, but I will increase the dose of metformin to 1500mg daily.
I will also recommend a home blood pressure monitor to track her blood pressure three times a week.
I will have a nutritionist come in to discuss dietary changes to reduce sodium intake.
I will schedule a follow-up appointment in one month to reassess her blood pressure and diabetes control.
